partisan_flags.quote: The architecture is not arrangement — it is design, and on this case the design held.
partisan_flags.why_biased: Applauds a right-wing, PAS/UMNO-led push for detention without trial and a royal decree banning an industry as a victory for constitutional procedure, demonstrating a strong conservative/establishment bias that validates authoritarian outcomes if they follow procedural steps.
partisan_flags.fix: Remove the editorial praise. State the political and legal outcomes neutrally without characterizing them as a triumph of constitutional design.

racial_flags.quote: The Sultan of Selangor's pig-farming ban took effect not on his decree date but on 12 February 2026...
racial_flags.why_biased: The text discusses a policy explicitly targeting a minority-dominated industry without once mentioning the affected community's views, the economic destruction of Chinese-owned farms, or the racial friction driving the ban. The affected minority is entirely stripped of agency.
racial_flags.fix: Explicitly note that the ban targets an industry primarily operated by and serving the Chinese/non-Muslim minority and was driven by Malay-majority objections in Tanjong Sepat and Bukit Tagar.

religious_flags.quote: The Selangor pig-farming sequence honoured both halves...
religious_flags.why_biased: Treats the total eradication of an agricultural sector to appease majority religious sensitivities as an unquestioned, harmonious good, ignoring the severe marginalization of non-Muslim rights.
religious_flags.fix: Address the tension between upholding majority religious sensitivities and protecting minority economic and cultural practices.

geographic_flags.quote: The Selangor case ran the route end to end.
geographic_flags.why_biased: While appropriate for a Selangor-focused card, it assumes the Selangor precedent of royal intervention into state economic planning is a neat template, without considering how this affects federal food security or other states.
geographic_flags.fix: Note the broader implications for federal food supply chains since Selangor will now rely 100% on imports.

economic_flags.quote: ...revised on 10 February 2026 to a total state ban with pork imports as the alternative;
economic_flags.why_biased: Gloss over the massive economic destruction of local businesses and the supply chain impact of forcing a state to rely entirely on imports for a staple protein.
economic_flags.fix: Include the economic cost to the Tanjong Sepat farmers whose operations and investments are being forcibly shut down.

omission_flags: The text completely omits the perspective, economic impact, or political voice of the pig farmers and the non-Muslim communities affected by the ban, reducing them to passive subjects of state regulation.

The text fails to mention the vocal opposition by DAP/Pakatan Harapan state representatives (such as Wong Siew Ki and Ronnie Liu) who defended the farmers, which was the actual catalyst for the PAS/UMNO 'Daulat Tuanku' rally.
evasion_flags: The text uses a sanitized, procedural tone to describe Ibrahim Ali's proposal, calling it a 'board-vote item' while burying the fact that it is an extremely draconian call for detention without trial reminiscent of the repealed Internal Security Act (ISA).

The text evades the question of whether a complete state-wide ban on pig farming via royal decree contradicts fundamental liberties or federal jurisdiction, treating the Exco adoption as the end of the legal discussion.
shoe_test_failures: If a non-Muslim NGO proposed 2-year preventive detention without trial for insulting a minority religion, the analysis would not sanitize it as merely a 'board-vote item properly addressed to Parliament'.

cardinal_sin_flags.quote: Think of it like a corporate chairman whose signature alone runs the day-to-day under the company's charter...
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The Federal Constitution (Article 71 and Eighth Schedule) explicitly requires the Ruler to act 'on the advice' of the Executive Council. The assertion that a constitutional monarch runs the day-to-day by 'signature alone' legally misrepresents Malaysian constitutional law.
cardinal_sin_flags.fix: Adjust the analogy to reflect the mandatory requirement for executive advice; the Sultan acts on the advice of the Exco, not by unilateral signature.

cardinal_sin_flags.quote: The Akta Kedaulatan Raja proposal would sit within this comparator landscape rather than create a category Malaysian law currently lacks.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The proposed Akta includes preventive detention without trial for up to two years. The cited international comparators (Thailand s.112, Spain, Denmark) are penal codes that require criminal trials. A law allowing detention without trial for speech is an extraordinary category that Malaysia *does* lack since the ISA's repeal, making this comparison deeply false.
cardinal_sin_flags.fix: Explicitly distinguish between standard penal lèse-majesté laws and preventive detention without trial, noting that the latter bypasses the judicial system entirely and represents a return to ISA-style powers.

cardinal_sin_flags.quote: The architecture is not arrangement — it is design, and on this case the design held.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Characterizing a racialized total ban on a minority agricultural sector and the proposal of an authoritarian detention law as the triumphant holding of constitutional 'design' applies a thick coat of establishment bias to highly contentious, rights-infringing developments.
cardinal_sin_flags.fix: Delete the editorializing. Present the legal mechanics without praising them as an elegant constitutional victory.

verification_log.claim: The Sultan of Selangor's pig-farming ban took effect not on his decree date but on 12 February 2026, when the State Executive Council formally adopted it.
verification_log.primary_source: CodeBlue (12 Feb 2026) quoting MB Amirudin Shari announcing the MMKN formal decision to cease licence issuance.

verification_log.claim: Sultan Sharafuddin issued three escalating decrees from 12 January to 7 May 2026.
verification_log.primary_source: Scoop (12 Jan 2026), CodeBlue (10 Feb 2026), The Star (8 May 2026 citing 7 May Royal Office statement).

verification_log.claim: On 16 May 2026 about 5,000 attended a Daulat Tuanku rally in Shah Alam.
verification_log.primary_source: RTM News / Berita RTM (16 May 2026) '5,000 sertai himpunan Daulat Tuanku'.

verification_log.claim: PUTRA's Ibrahim Ali publicly proposed an Akta Kedaulatan Raja. Reported provisions include detention without warrant for up to two weeks and preventive detention up to two years, plus a Royal Sovereignty Council.
verification_log.primary_source: Harakahdaily (17 May 2026) and Dhomir (13 May 2026) publishing Ibrahim Ali's exact press release detailing '14 hari tanpa waran mahkamah' and 'tahan selama 2 tahun tanpa bicara'.

verification_log.claim: The Centre for Independent Journalism recorded 233 arrests and investigations under expression-restricting laws in 2025, up from 189 in 2024.
verification_log.primary_source: CIJ 'Report on the State of Freedom of Expression in Malaysia 2025' (12 Dec 2025).

verification_log.claim: Lèse-majesté statutes exist in Thailand (Penal Code s.112, 3 to 15 years)...
Selangor PAS Commissioner Ab Halim Tamuri and Selangor UMNO chief Megat Zulkarnain Omardin attended.
verification_log.primary_source: Utusan Malaysia (16 May 2026) 'Pemuda Pas Selangor tubuh Gerakan Daulat' explicitly naming both leaders.

overall_assessment: The analysis exhibits severe procedural masking, using dry constitutional language to sanitize two highly controversial, racially charged developments. By framing the total eradication of Selangor's pig farming industry and a proposed ISA-style detention-without-trial law as elegant triumphs of 'constitutional design,' the author commits grave omission and evasion biases. The text completely erases the non-Muslim minority affected by the ban, ignores the political opposition that sparked the 'Daulat Tuanku' rally, and falsely equates an authoritarian preventive detention proposal with standard European lèse-majesté penal codes. Furthermore, the corporate chairman analogy legally misrepresents the Federal Constitution by claiming the Ruler can run state affairs 'by signature alone' without Exco advice.
